Same outward behavior as s08: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in, agent reply out.
The only externally-visible change is the new `[s09] hook recorded ...`
line printed after Run returns, summarizing what SDKCaptureHook captured.

$ go run . -workspace /tmp/agent-demo -skills weather -v "Tokyo weather"
[s09] bootstrap files present: 0/4 ([AGENTS.md SOUL.md USER.md TOOLS.md])
[s09] loaded 2 skill(s) from workspace="/tmp/agent-demo/skills" builtin=".../testdata/skills"
       - echo
       - weather
[s09] skills requested for system prompt: [weather]
[s09] system prompt: 612 chars; messages: 1
[iter 0] -> bash {command:curl -s wttr.in/Tokyo?format=3}
[iter 0] <- Tokyo: ☀ +18°C
[iter 0] assistant: ...
[s09] hook recorded 1 tool invocation(s): [bash]
Tokyo: clear, 18°C.

The five hook tap points fire, in this order, per iteration:

  BeforeIter(0)
  (LLM call: returns tool_calls)
  BeforeExecuteTools(0)
  (bash runs)
  AfterIter(0)
  BeforeIter(1)
  (LLM call: returns stop)
  AfterIter(1)
  FinalizeContent

If a hook in CompositeHook panics:

  CompositeHook recovers it, logs via LogPanic, then surfaces an error
  to the Runner. Other registered hooks still run for the same call.

If BeforeExecuteTools returns an error:

  Runner bails mid-iteration. The tool body never runs. AfterIter still
  fires so observability hooks see the failed iteration. The error is
  wrapped and surfaced from Run.

If FinalizeContent rewrites the content:

  AgentRunResult.FinalContent is the rewritten string. The caller can't
  tell from the result struct that a hook intervened — that's the whole
  point.

Tests:

$ go test -count=1 -race ./...
ok      learn-nanobot/s09       0.5s

The 6 tests pin down: call order across a 2-turn loop, error-isolation
in CompositeHook, SDKCaptureHook accumulation, FinalizeContent rewrite
path, BeforeExecuteTools short-circuit, and the no-hook (default Noop)
sanity check.
